name: Whole Grains (barley, farro, oats, brown rice)

book_claims.claim: Regular consumption prevents chronic disease and supports longevity
book_claims.mechanism: Rich in vegetable fibre that feeds gut microbes to produce protective metabolites; provides vitamins, oligo-elements and phytochemicals

book_claims.claim: Regular consumption reduces cardiovascular disease risk by 20-40% and diabetes risk by 20-30%
book_claims.mechanism: Fibre-rich cell wall restricts rate of starch/sugar digestion and absorption; slow glucose release reduces insulin spikes; low glycaemic index induces satiety

book_claims.claim: Highest intake of wholegrain fibre associated with 19% lower all-cause mortality and 15-34% lower disease-specific mortality
book_claims.mechanism: Fibre increases stool bulk, increases bile acid excretion, alters colonic flora, and generates short-chain fatty acids
book_claims.study_ref: Ref 90 in book (NIH-AARP Diet and Health Study, 367,442 participants)

book_claims.claim: Two servings per day associated with 21% reduction in type 2 diabetes risk
book_claims.mechanism: Beta-glucan fibre lowers glucose, insulin and cholesterol by reducing intestinal absorption

book_claims.claim: Three to five servings daily associated with 21% lower cardiovascular disease risk
book_claims.mechanism: Multiple pathways including cholesterol reduction and anti-inflammatory effects

book_claims.claim: Bran contains phyto-protectants with antioxidative and anticarcinogenic activities. Germ contains spermidine which extends lifespan in animal models.
book_claims.mechanism: Ferulic acid, alkylresorcinols, apigenin, lignans, phytic acid in bran; spermidine in germ removes damaged cells and reduces inflammation

supplementary_research.finding: Meta-analysis (Aune et al., BMJ 2016) of 45 studies found each 90g/day increment of whole grains associated with 17% lower all-cause mortality, 22% lower CVD mortality, and 15% lower cancer mortality in a dose-response relationship.

Lancet meta-analysis (Reynolds et al., 2019) of 185 prospective studies found highest vs lowest whole grain intake associated with 13-33% reduction in CVD, diabetes, cancer, and respiratory disease, with 25-29g fibre/day being optimal.

Intervention study (Eisenberg et al., 2016; confirmed in humans 2021) demonstrated that spermidine from whole grain germ activates autophagy and improves cardiovascular function, supporting the book's claim about germ-derived longevity compounds.

culinary_pairings.source: Complementary amino acids for complete protein
Olive oil slows carbohydrate absorption from grains
Combined fibre sources for microbiome diversity
Allium prebiotic fibres complement grain fibres

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Oats and barley richest in beta-glucan (3-8g/100g), which forms gel in intestine slowing glucose absorption. Intact grain structure preserves slow-release properties.
Autophagy activator; wheat germ is one of the richest dietary sources. Refining removes most spermidine content.
Bound to bran; released by gut microbiota fermentation. Lignans converted to enterolignans with anti-cancer properties.

synergies.description: Complementary amino acid profiles; combined provide complete protein
Olive oil slows carbohydrate absorption from grains
